assumptions: This scaffold uses deterministic planning instead of live provider calls.
Future versions will route across external deep research providers and internal tools.
Artifacts are the primary delivery surface; chat text is secondary.

steps.title: Scope task
steps.description: Interpret the natural-language request and derive the execution scope.

steps.title: Collect evidence
steps.description: Select providers and gather raw sources for the run.

steps.title: Process and normalize
steps.description: Clean, deduplicate, and organize source material into structured findings.

steps.title: Deliver artifacts
steps.description: Render report and machine-readable outputs for downstream workflows.

findings.title: Agent-native execution model
findings.detail: The product should treat the request as a run with explicit planning, execution state, and exportable artifacts instead of a chat-only interaction.

findings.title: Artifact-first delivery contract
findings.detail: Every run should emit a manifest, report, structured findings, and a source ledger so results can be audited and reused by teams.

findings.title: General workflow fit
findings.detail: This request does not yet map to a specialized flow. The runtime should fall back to generic planning and then route into collection, processing, and delivery.

findings.title: Task captured
findings.detail: The current run objective is: robotics industry overview

findings.title: No evidence attached
findings.detail: This run did not include explicit files or URLs. It still produced artifacts, but live research providers need to be connected for evidence-backed output.
